description: Evaluates whether the design-fit-reviewer skill catches layering violations and duplication while ignoring config-only changes and legitimate in-layer code.

cases.name: sql-in-http-handler
cases.notes: HTTP handler now issues raw SQL directly instead of going through the users repository. Layering violation; the skill should flag the routes/repo boundary crossing.

cases.name: duplicated-format-date-helper
cases.notes: Inline comment explicitly notes the existing helper in src/util/date.ts. Re-implementing it in the reports module is duplication; the skill should flag it.

cases.name: config-only-pr
cases.diff:
diff --git a/.eslintrc.json b/.eslintrc.json
index 5555eee..6666fff 100644
--- a/.eslintrc.json
+++ b/.eslintrc.json
@@ -6,6 +6,8 @@
   "rules": {
     "no-unused-vars": "error",
     "prefer-const": "error",
+    "eqeqeq": ["error", "always"],
+    "no-console": "warn",
     "semi": ["error", "always"]
   }
 }
cases.notes: Pure linter configuration change. Design-fit is about architectural placement and duplication, neither of which applies to a config-only PR.

cases.name: legitimate-repo-layer-addition
cases.notes: SQL in the repo layer is exactly where it belongs. No layering violation and not a duplicate - it's a sibling function.
